The text states: "For the overall survival analyses, the median follow-up periods from randomisation to the cutoff date of June 1, 2021, were 4·41 years for the overall population". Convert 4.41 years to months (4.41 * 12 = 52.92 months).
Locate the "Methods" section in Chunk 5. The last sentence of the "Methods" paragraph states: "This study is ongoing and is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov, NCT01957436."
I have examined Chunk 5 (pages 1-2) for any mention of a PubMed ID (PMID) in the publication details or header/footer, and it is not present. The provided identifiers are DOIs.
The title of the article in Chunk 5 explicitly mentions "(PEACE-1)" as the trial name. The "Methods" section also refers to it as "(PEACE-1)".
The author list is provided at the very beginning of the article in Chunk 5. The first author is Karim Fizazi.
In Chunk 5, below the title, it states "Published Online April 8, 2022". The year is 2022.

The document includes a detailed "Summary," "Methods," "Findings," "Interpretation," and "Discussion" section, along with multiple tables and figures, indicating it is a full publication rather than an abstract.

The title of the article in Chunk 5 states "phase 3 study". The "Methods" section also explicitly states: "We conducted an open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2x2 factorial design (PEACE-1)".

In Chunk 5, the "Methods" section states, "Abiraterone efficacy was first assessed in the overall population and then in the population who received androgen deprivation therapy with docetaxel as standard of care (population of interest). This study is ongoing..." In Chunk 6, the "Added value of this study" box states, "To our knowledge, PEACE-1 is the first trial to show that a triple systemic therapy...". The "Statistical analysis" section in Chunk 6 also mentions, "The coprimary endpoint results presented here correspond to the final planned analysis; no interim analysis was conducted." These statements indicate this is the original report of the primary findings.

On page 1, in the "Methods" section, the text states: "Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone." This lists four distinct arms.

In Figure 1 (Chunk 0, page 5), locate the box for "583 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) plus abiraterone groups". The number of patients listed as "deceased at data cutoff" is 228. This is also stated in the "Findings" section of the Summary (Chunk 5, page 1) and the "Results" section (Chunk 7, page 5): "the addition of abiraterone to SOC (with or without docetaxel and with or without radiotherapy) decreased the number of deaths from 268 to 228". The number 228 corresponds to the abiraterone (treatment) group.

In Figure 1 (Chunk 0, page 5), locate the box for "589 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) without abiraterone groups". The number of patients listed as "deceased at data cutoff" is 268. This is also stated in the "Findings" section of the Summary (Chunk 5, page 1) and the "Results" section (Chunk 7, page 5): "the addition of abiraterone to SOC (with or without docetaxel and with or without radiotherapy) decreased the number of deaths from 268 to 228". The number 268 corresponds to the without abiraterone (control) group.

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The study explicitly states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma' (page 1, Methods section, Chunk 5). 'De novo' refers to synchronous metastases. The Interpretation section further clarifies: 'Nonetheless, as PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC, it is still unclear whether this triple therapy might benefit patients with metachronous mCSPC' (page 11, Chunk 5). This indicates that 100% of the patients in the study, across all treatment arms, had synchronous metastases.

This indicates that 100% of the patients in the study, across all control arms, had synchronous metastases.

Chunk 5 (Summary and Findings), Chunk 6 (Introduction and Methods), Chunk 7 (Results and Discussion), and Chunk 3 (Table 2: Efficacy outcomes) were examined. The text states: "The secondary endpoints were castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC)-free survival, serious-genito-urinary-event-free survival, prostate-cancer-specificsurvival, time to next skeletal-related event, PSA response rate, prognostic study of serum PSA measured 6-8 months after initiation of systemic therapy, time to pain progression, time to chemotherapy for CRPC, quality of life, changes in bone mineral density, correlation of biomarkers with outcome, event rate per 100 person-years of treatment analysis, and toxicity."

On page 1, in the "Findings" section, the text states: "1173 patients were enrolled... and assigned to receive standard of care (n=296)". Figure 1 on page 5, under "1173 patients enrolled and randomly assigned", shows "296 assigned to SOC".

In the overall population, patients assigned to receive abiraterone had a hazard ratio (HR) for radiographic progression-free survival of 0.54 (99.9% CI 0.41–0.71; p<0.0001) compared to patients who did not receive abiraterone. This HR represents the treatment effect. No single percentage rate for a specific time point is explicitly reported in the text or tables for the treatment arm alone.

I examined Chunk 5 (Findings section, page 1) and Chunk 3 (Table 2, page 9) for a hazard ratio or a specific percentage rate for progression-free survival in the overall control arm. The hazard ratio of 0.54 (99.9% CI 0.41–0.71) is a comparative measure of the treatment arm versus the control arm, not a rate or HR of the control arm itself. No single percentage rate for a specific time point is explicitly reported in the text or tables for the control arm alone.

Extracted from the text on Page 1, Summary, Background, Methods, and Interpretation sections. The treatments are classified as Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT), Docetaxel (chemotherapy), Second-generation hormonal therapy / androgen receptor axis inhibitors (specifically abiraterone plus prednisone), Radiotherapy, and Triple systemic therapy (a combination of ADT, docetaxel, and abiraterone/prednisone).
The types of therapy are explicitly described in the 'Methods' section and further detailed in the 'Procedures' subsection. The value is present on page 1 and page 3.

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: In Table 1, under the "Metastatic burdens" row, for "High burden": For the "Overall population (n=1172)" and "SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=583)" column, extract "331 (57%)". For the "ADT with docetaxel population (n=710)" and "SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)" column, extract "224 (63%)".

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Control
columns.extraction_plan: In Table 1, under the "Metastatic burdens" row, for "High burden": For the "Overall population (n=1172)" and "SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=589)" column, extract "336 (57%)". For the "ADT with docetaxel population (n=710)" and "SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)" column, extract "232 (65%)".

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Control

For the "ADT with docetaxel population (n=710)" and "SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)" column, extract "123 (35%)".
